readinessHeadline: AI recovery readiness is weaker than the current board packet implies.
recoverySignal: The approval path reopens cleanly only when the same people are available to absorb policy and security collisions.
weakestFallback: Policy-review backup path during an AI exception burst

readinessHeadline: Identity recovery windows are too long for the next audit-driven cycle.
recoverySignal: The path stays open, but recovery time grows quickly once audit escalations queue behind security approvals.
weakestFallback: Audit exception restore path during stacked reviews

readinessHeadline: Revenue recovery still depends on one narrow signoff route.
recoverySignal: The board packet shows who reviewed the decision, but not how the path recovers when one approver stalls.
weakestFallback: Commercial signoff backup during forecast reopen

readinessHeadline: FinTech recovery readiness is now a buffer-capacity problem.
recoverySignal: Merchant, treasury, and KYC reviews still converge on one overloaded recovery route.
weakestFallback: Parallel diligence restore path during merchant-risk and KYC spikes

readinessHeadline: Procurement remains the strongest recovery reference path in the estate.
recoverySignal: Trust and commercial packets close with enough redundancy and named fallback coverage to absorb more demand.
weakestFallback: None immediately visible under current modeled load

readinessHeadline: Biotech recovery readiness is weaker than regulated packets suggest.
recoverySignal: CAPA, specimen, and safety packets still rely on narrow restore routes with weak fallback ownership.
weakestFallback: Quality and compliance restore path during the next regulated exception burst
